task_objective: Add a persisted per-game Sync This Game opt-out that blocks automatic launch restore, exit backup, launch pause, and Syncthing watches while preserving global Automatic Sync behavior and showing a start-only informational notice.
tests_added: Atomic settings mutation covers first write, sorted and de-duplicated membership, multi-instance merge preservation, re-enable behavior, fail-closed read errors, lock timeouts, per-entry load validation, and reload round trips.

All five automatic lifecycle entry points reject disabled games, global Automatic Sync remains authoritative, enabled games are unchanged, a late disable blocks backup_game_on_exit, and disabled skips do not record history.
Decky settings and RPC contract tests verify the new persisted key and async backend wrapper.

Frontend state tests cover malformed settings, sorted optimistic membership, numeric Steam IDs, aliases, exact names, eligible short-name fuzzy matching, ambiguous matches, empty registries, and the Hades versus Hades II app-ID case.

Mutation tests cover optimistic success, hydrated scoped rollback, independent A-success/B-failure, timeout with late success, different-game rapid toggles, and same-game disable-success/re-enable-failure convergence.
Lifecycle controller tests prove a disabled game performs no pause, initial pre-game watch, conflict-restart watch, or post-game watch while an enabled game retains all four behaviors.
Status tests verify the exact disabled notice, amber slash icon, normal auto-hide, start publication, explicit exit hide, exit pre-check suppression, and exclusion from silent skip reasons.
design_decisions: Per-game state is an opt-out set keyed by sanitized canonical Ludusavi game name; absence therefore preserves the enabled default for existing installations.
PersistenceManager.mutate_settings performs one settings-only, fail-closed transaction so a sibling backend instance cannot erase another game's opt-out during Decky reload overlap.
Frontend canonical resolution follows backend priority: Steam app ID, alias, normalized exact name, then one backend-eligible fuzzy candidate; unresolved names conservatively retain launch guarding.
Per-game mutation sequencing is keyed by game name, every successful RPC refreshes the persisted cache, and rollback changes only the failed game using cache state at failure time.
Disabled games still call lifecycle check RPCs so the backend remains authoritative and can drive the start notice, but they never pause or create Syncthing watches.
game_sync_disabled is intentionally non-silent: start publishes the informational result, while exit explicitly hides the status and suppresses the frontend checking flash.

results.automated: The task-specific suites passed through the implementation commits, culminating in 304 frontend tests, TypeScript checking, frontend build/package validation, Ruff check/format, ty, and 838 Python tests at 88.69% coverage. The orchestration quality gate is run again after this documentation commit before the round-complete marker is written.
results.release: No release, tag, push, or merge is part of this implementation round; approved orchestration finalization owns those actions.
results.on_device: Deferred and not claimed as passed. Steam Deck verification must confirm toggle placement and selected-game updates, persistence through reload/force-quit and multi-game toggles, start-only notice auto-hide, no visible launch pause or restore, no exit backup/post-game watch/history/status strip, app-ID/alias/substring resolution parity, no notice when global Automatic Sync is off, and the new startup_settings_hydrated and App started log fields.
